summary.intent: Implement a lazy tool registry mode that shows a single meta-tool (`tool_search`) instead of all tool schemas each LLM turn, dramatically reducing token usage when many MCP servers are connected.
summary.outcome: All four phases implemented and merged into PR #467: ToolRegistry plumbing with activated map, ToolSearchTool + wrap_registry_lazy, config wiring (ToolRegistryMode enum), system prompt hints, and docs. PR review feedback addressed in a follow-up commit.

repo: The worktree branch `honeysuckle-grapple` is based on a local `main` that is 1566 commits ahead of `origin/main` ‚Äî PRs must be rebased onto `origin/main` before pushing.
Chat dispatch integration point for post-filter registry wrapping is in `crates/chat/src/lib.rs` around line 5994, inside the `run_with_tools` function using `persona.config` for config access.
ToolRegistry uses `Arc<dyn AgentTool>` internally via `ToolEntry`; the `get()` method returns `Arc<dyn AgentTool>` which derefs correctly for async `.execute()` calls.
Config schema map for TOML validation lives in `crates/config/src/validate.rs` `build_schema_map()` and must be updated when adding new config fields.
The `beads`/dolt issue tracking tool was attempted but failed to start ‚Äî the project uses a dolt-backed issue tracker that was not available in this environment.
Clippy flagged a `map_or` suggestion on an `Option` chain in `tool_registry.rs` that needed fixing before merge.

code.finding: Added `ActivatedTools = Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<dyn AgentTool>>>>` field; `get()` checks static tools first then activated map; `list_schemas()` and `list_names()` append activated entries; clone methods produce fresh empty activated sets; `get_arc()` was removed in PR review ‚Äî callers migrated to `get()`.

Both `run_agent_loop_with_context` and `run_agent_loop_streaming` had `tool_schemas`/`schemas_for_api` moved from before the loop to inside it, so newly activated tools appear on the next iteration. Max iterations multiplied by 3 when lazy mode detected (via config, not `has_activated_tools()` after PR review fix).

NEW file: `ToolSearchTool` scores matches as name-exact=100, name-contains=50, word-overlap=10√ócount, caps at 15 results. `wrap_registry_lazy()` shares the activated Arc between the new registry and ToolSearchTool. Returns `Err` (not empty Ok) when called with no params (fixed in PR review). Source metadata preserved on activation after PR review.
`ToolRegistryMode` enum added with `#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]` and `#[default] Full` variant; added as `registry_mode` field on `ToolsConfig`.
Lazy mode detected by checking if `tool_search` is in schemas but `memory_search` is not; injects a hint telling the model to use `tool_search` to discover memory tools.

workflow: When a worktree branch diverges from remote main, `git rebase origin/main` with a single commit on top works cleanly ‚Äî the warnings about skipped commits are benign when the commit replays cleanly.
Force push (`git push --force-with-lease`) is required after rebase to update the remote tracking branch before PR creation.
PR review fixes were batched into a single follow-up commit rather than interactive rebase amending ‚Äî cleaner history for review.
Running `cargo clippy` after `cargo fmt` caught a `map_or` lint before merge.

friction: Local `main` was 1566 commits ahead of `origin/main`, causing `gh pr create` to fail with no common history ‚Äî required rebase onto `origin/main` before the PR could be created.
The `beads`/dolt issue tracker tool failed to start and could not be used for task tracking.
Initial `gh pr create` call failed silently; needed explicit `--base origin/main` flag and investigation of remote state to diagnose.
open_items: Manual QA: set `registry_mode = "lazy"` in moltis.toml, connect an MCP server with 50+ tools, and verify the model uses `tool_search` to discover and call tools correctly.
Token comparison benchmark: measure input tokens per turn in full vs lazy mode with 50+ MCP tools to quantify the improvement.
Phase 3+4 are intentionally in the same PR (not split as the plan suggested for PR2) ‚Äî the plan's PR strategy of splitting config wiring into a separate PR was collapsed.
Sub-agents do not inherit the activated tools map (fresh empty map on clone) ‚Äî if sub-agent tool inheritance is ever needed, this will require revisiting the clone behavior.
